item.title: KKR, Capital Group to Start Public-Private Credit Fund in Asia
item.description: KKR & Co. and Capital Group plan to launch a public-private credit fund in Asia this year, in the latest tie-up between private-markets specialists and traditional asset managers seeking to attract more retail money.

item.title: Australia Debt Office Urged to Boost Staffing, Lower Cash Buffer
item.description: Australia’s sovereign debt manager should bolster staffing and consider lowering its large cash buffer to maintain best-practice as issuances grow, according to an independent Treasury-commissioned review that outlined potential risks to the nation’s funding strategy.

item.title: Base Metals Slide as Iran Uncertainty Keeps Pressure on Prices
item.description: Copper headed for a weekly loss, tracked by other metals, as uncertainty over the next developments in the Middle East war continued to weigh on the global economy.

item.title: SBI Funds Is Said to Start Marketing $1.5 Billion IPO Next Week
item.description: SBI Funds Management Ltd. is set to begin formal marketing as early as next week for its planned initial public offering to raise as much as $1.5 billion, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Central Asia Gains as War Shakes Energy Markets and Trade Routes
item.description: Central Asia, once treated as a geopolitical backwater, is seizing the spotlight as international capital flows and foreign powers compete for access to its vast mineral and energy wealth.

item.title: ECB to Hike in June Before Reversing Course in 2027, Poll Shows
item.description: The European Central Bank will respond to the Iran war by raising interest rates once — in June — before reversing the move next year to protect economic growth, analysts say.

item.title: Warburg Pincus-Backed PDG Is Said to Tap Goldman for Stake Sale
item.description: Asian data center operator Princeton Digital Group has tapped Goldman Sachs Group Inc. to work on a strategic review that may result in a stake sale, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: US-Sanctioned Supertanker With Iranian Oil Tries Hormuz Transit
item.description: A US-sanctioned supertanker laden with Iranian oil appeared to be attempting to cross the Strait of Hormuz on Friday, with traffic through the waterway otherwise at a virtual standstill.

item.title: Italians’ Fear of Buying Stocks Is Costing the Country Billions
item.description: Italy is locked into a failing investment strategy that is costing families billions of euros and holding back efforts to get their economy growing.

item.title: SNB Move Below Zero Bigger Step Than Normal Cut: Schlegel Tells NZZ
item.description: Swiss National Bank President Martin Schlegel said cutting borrowing costs below zero is a more significant move than a reduction above that level, according to an interview in Neue Zuercher Zeitung.

item.title: China’s First Special Bond Sale of the Year Draws Strong Demand
item.description: China saw strong demand at its first ultra-long special bond sale of the year, with 30-year yields reaching the lowest level since November.

item.title: BYD, Geely See More EV Demand As Oil Prices Climb From Iran War
item.description: China’s BYD Co. and Geely Automobile Holdings Ltd. are poised to benefit from higher demand for electric vehicles as the Iran war drives up oil prices.

item.title: World’s Biggest Lithium Mine Cuts Full-Year Output Guidance
item.description: Shares in Australian miner IGO Ltd. tumbled as much as 14% after the company delivered a major downgrade to production guidance for Greenbushes, the world’s biggest hard-rock lithium mine.

item.title: Singapore’s Growth-Inflation Quandary Set to Sharpen on Iran War
item.description: Singapore’s central bank faces a sharper growth-inflation tradeoff due to the Iran-war-driven energy shock — splitting economists on whether it will tighten again in July to counter higher import prices or stand pat to shield economic activity.

item.title: DeepSeek Unveils Flagship AI Model a Year After Breakthrough
item.description: DeepSeek rolled out preview versions of a new flagship artificial intelligence model a year after upending Silicon Valley, calling it the most powerful open-source platform in a challenge to rivals from OpenAI to Anthropic PBC.

item.title: Seraya Said to Mull Sale of Offshore Wind Vessel Operator Cyan
item.description: Seraya Partners is considering selling Cyan Renewables Pte, an operator of vessels that serve the offshore wind industry, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Russia Set to Cut Key Rate Amid Economic Woes and Iran-War Risks
item.description: Russia’s central bank is poised to continue cutting interest rates as the economy loses steam, though uncertainty over the budget and the fallout from the Middle East conflict is limiting how far policymakers are willing to go.

item.title: Poor Mutual Fund Returns to Put Indian Investors’ Loyalty to the Test
item.description: So far, retail investors are staying the course despite war-led losses in March.

item.title: TSMC Shares Surge as Taiwan Lifts Single-Stock Limit for Funds
item.description: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. shares climbed after Taiwan’s financial regulator eased limits on single-stock fund holdings, a move that JPMorgan Chase & Co. said could draw more than $6 billion in new inflows.

item.title: Philippine Central Bank Signals Series of Modest Rate Hikes
item.description: The Philippine central bank will likely deliver a series of modest increases in its benchmark interest rate to rein in inflation spurred by the global oil shock, according to Governor Eli Remolona.

item.title: TPG-Led Consortium to Buy India’s Top Green Finance Shadow Bank
item.description: A consortium led by TPG Inc. has signed a formal agreement to buy India’s top shadow lender specializing in green financing, underscoring foreign investors’ appetite for the country’s fast-growing environmental projects.

item.title: India’s Currency Is ‘Undervalued,’ Top Economic Official Says
item.description: India’s rupee is “fundamentally undervalued,” the country’s chief economic adviser said, adding that current levels could draw investors.

item.title: Collapsing Volatility Turbocharges Returns Across Carry Trades
item.description: DoubleLine Capital and Van Eck Associates Corp. are among investors seeing renewed appeal in a currency strategy that’s revving up as the Middle East ceasefire helps steady markets and reignite risk appetite.

item.title: Canon Shares Drop After Guidance Cut as Higher Memory Costs Bite
item.description: Canon shares fell as much as 7.6% in Tokyo after the camera maker cut its earnings guidance citing the impact of rising memory chip prices.

item.title: Amazon-Backed Nuclear Firm X-Energy Raises $1.02 Billion in IPO
item.description: X-Energy Inc., a nuclear energy firm that counts Amazon.com Inc. as a backer, raised $1.02 billion in an upsized US initial public offering that priced above the marketed range.

item.title: China’s Carmakers Grab 30% of Europe’s Plug-In Hybrid Sales
item.description: Chinese automakers made strong inroads into Europe last month as consumers rushed to buy more affordable plug-in hybrids from brands led by BYD Co.

item.title: AI Coding Firm Cognition in Funding Talks at $25 Billion Value
item.description: Cognition AI Inc. is in early talks to raise a new round of funding that would more than double its valuation to $25 billion, according to people familiar with the matter, tapping into increased demand for companies that understand how to use artificial intelligence in software development.

item.title: China Tech Split Emerges as ChiNext Rally Beats Hong Kong Peer
item.description: Mainland‑listed Chinese tech stocks are outperforming their Hong Kong peers, underscoring investors’ enthusiasm for artificial intelligence hardware and earnings visibility.

item.title: Japan’s Inflation Picks Up as Oil Clouds Outlook Ahead of BOJ
item.description: Japan’s key inflation indicator accelerated for the first time in five months, suggesting price pressures were building even before the full impact of elevated oil prices ripples through the economy.

item.title: Inflation Looms as China’s Exporters Boost Prices on Iran Costs
item.description: Chinese exporters are beginning to lift prices on everything from swimsuits to air conditioners as the Iran war drives up oil-linked input costs, signaling that global consumer goods inflation is likely to accelerate.
